task.name: Security Gate — Universal Security Awareness
task.objective: Enforce security-first thinking across ALL agents, not just the security specialist. Every agent is a security agent.

llm.mandate: Security is not Shield's job alone — EVERY agent must think security-first
Never trade security for speed. If unsure, flag it.

universal-checklist.check: Am I handling user input? → Validate, sanitize, parameterize. NEVER trust input.
Does this touch auth flows? → Verify session handling, token management, credential storage.
Does this access data? → Verify the user/tenant has permission. Check RLS, RBAC, scoping.
Am I exposing data? → Check for PII leakage, overly broad API responses, logging sensitive data.
Are there secrets involved? → NEVER hardcode. Use env vars, secret managers, .env (gitignored).
Am I adding a dependency? → Check for known vulnerabilities, license compatibility, maintenance status.
How do errors surface? → NEVER expose stack traces, internal paths, or system info to users.
What am I logging? → NEVER log passwords, tokens, PII, or full request bodies with sensitive data.
Am I building queries/commands? → Parameterize everything. No string concatenation for SQL, shell, or LDAP.
Is data moving between systems? → HTTPS only. Verify TLS. Check certificate pinning where applicable.

agent.name: dev
agent.responsibility: Write secure code. Follow OWASP Top 10. Parameterize all queries. Never expose internals in error messages. Input validation at every boundary.

agent.name: architect
agent.responsibility: Design for security from the start. Threat model every new component. Defense in depth. Principle of least privilege in all access patterns.

agent.name: tea
agent.responsibility: Write security-focused test cases. Test for injection, auth bypass, privilege escalation. Include negative test scenarios.

agent.name: devops
agent.responsibility: Secure the pipeline. No secrets in CI configs. Container scanning. Image pinning. Network policies. Least-privilege IAM.

agent.name: api-contract
agent.responsibility: Validate all API contracts include auth requirements. Check for data leakage in response schemas. Rate limiting on all endpoints.

agent.name: data-architect
agent.responsibility: RLS on every table. Encryption at rest. Audit logging for data access. No direct DB access from frontend.

agent.name: tech-writer
agent.responsibility: Document security requirements in user-facing docs. Never include internal URLs, credentials, or system architecture details in public docs.

agent.name: pm
agent.responsibility: Include security requirements in every PRD. Consider threat scenarios in user stories. Non-functional security requirements are first-class.

agent.name: ux-designer
agent.responsibility: Design secure user flows. No security info in URLs. Proper session timeout UX. Clear permission denied states.

agent.name: custodian
agent.responsibility: Scan for security anti-patterns in repo. Flag hardcoded secrets, missing input validation, disabled security features.

escalation.level: Agent notices a minor security issue in their own work. → Fix it immediately. Note in handoff document.
Agent notices a security issue outside their scope. → Write a [SECURITY] finding to team/_memory/_comms/findings/. → Tanto triages and assigns to appropriate agent.
Agent encounters a complex security concern needing expert analysis. → Flag to Tanto with recommendation to deploy Shield (Security Auditor). → Tanto invokes /team:security-auditor with the finding.

CRITICAL security vulnerability, data breach risk, or compliance violation. → Tanto immediately surfaces to CEO via the CEO Approval Gate. → ALL work on affected component HALTS until CEO acknowledges.

enforcement.rule: Before any code review (CR workflow), the reviewer MUST run the universal security checklist against all changed files
Before any ship (SH workflow), verify no new security warnings were introduced
New dependencies require a security check: known CVEs, last update date, maintainer reputation
Any agent creating a hook MUST consider security implications of the hook's commands
Any agent spawning a helper MUST scope the helper's permissions to minimum necessary
